title: Vision Transformer (ViT)
summary: Replaced convolutions with self-attention for image processing. Image split into patches (16×16), each patch treated as a token with a linear embedding. Standard transformer encoder applied. Showed that with enough data, transformers outperform CNNs on vision — less inductive bias compensated by more data.
key_insights: Tokenization: split image into fixed-size patches → flatten → linear projection to d_model. Patches = tokens.
Position embedding is learned (not sine/cosine) — 2D position info for image patches
[CLS] token prepended for classification output (same as BERT)
Key result: CNNs have strong local bias (good for small data), ViT has weak bias (needs large data but achieves higher ceiling)
Directly connects to tokenization decisions for non-language data (your sensor/tabular interest)

mathematical_core.patch_embedding: Image [H,W,C] → patches [N, P²·C] → linear projection [N, d_model]. N = HW/P² patches.
mathematical_core.architecture: Standard transformer encoder (same blocks as TRANSFORMER.ENCODER_DECODER encoder side)

related: TRANSFORMER.VARIANTS — ViT is encoder-only transformer for vision
FOUNDATIONS.INDUCTIVE_BIAS — less bias + more data vs more bias + less data tradeoff

notes: Learning plan Week 5 topic. Tokenization strategy for images directly parallels tokenization decisions for time series and tabular data.
